title: The Virginia Tech Massacre... What Really Happened
body: Nikki Giovanni is a renowned poet and author. I completely forgot that she taught at Virginia Tech and taught Seung. It seems like there were plenty of opportunities to get him some help. He definitely wasn’t well enough to be a student there. I’m glad Virginia Tech has put policies in place to help prevent situations like this. RIP to all the students and staff members who lost their lives 😞🕊️.
author: @Sincerely_ShyJ

title: The Virginia Tech Massacre... What Really Happened
body: This is my Alma mater. We will always remember this day. For years we never had class on 4/16, and instead, a day of remembrance and candlelight vigil. I was on campus for the second shooting, in 2011. I’ll never forget missing the alert text, walking back to my dorm, and a swat officer screaming at me “do you want to die?! Run!” And frantically trying to get into my dorm.. banging the doors to be let in and eventually was. Such a scary moment. My heart will always cry for the victims in these tragedies. But one thing that makes Virginia Tech so special is that our school spirit is unwavering. We stand together. Hokies forever 🦃 ❤
author: @Mooniemagic21

title: The Virginia Tech Massacre... What Really Happened

title: The Virginia Tech Massacre... What Really Happened
body: Just an fyi, the email that his professor sent to him says "You're paper of October 10 is 'disturbing' to me" not "...is disgusting to me"
author: @Toldyasogirl

title: The Virginia Tech Massacre... What Really Happened
body:
great video. after watching this, i ended up reading more about him on Wikipedia and found the transcript interview done in '07, to his flatmates, where they said that he would play on repeat "Shine Down" by Collective Soul («Teach me how to speak, teach me how to share, teach me where to go. Tell me, will love be there? Will love be there?»)...and it's just all in all too sad. he was a troubled kid, that fortunately had a loving family that did everything possible to improve his wellbeing and mental health but it was still not enough. he clearly needed a more intensive kind of treatment to help him overcome his selective mutism and major depression. despite all of this, can't help myself to feel disgusted by how he chose to show his frustration with himself and the world. because even though it was found that the system failed him in numerous ways as well (check the aftermath's section on Seung's wiki), his greatest enemy was himself.

hope everyone impacted by this tragedy finds peace!
author: @DoIReallyHaveTo

title: The Virginia Tech Massacre... What Really Happened
body: Haven't finished watching the video yet but I wanna give a huge shout-out to the parents for putting him in, and encouraging him to go to therapy, especially since mental health is often viewed as a shameful topic in Korean culture (I have a friend who was born and lives there. Total awesome dude, I've learned a lot more about their culture because of him, at least through his perspective). Although the view on it is progressing to being more positive, there's still a lot of work to be done.

title: The Virginia Tech Massacre... What Really Happened
body: Joel, thank you for this. I watch a lot of true crime (I originally wanted to be a Homicide Detective with the L.A. Sheriff's Dept. when I was about 20), but this is definitely the most thorough, comprehensive and well done deep dive I have ever seen. FYI, I have been a professional Anthropologist/Archaeologist in Mexico for 44 years (PhD finally in 2000, from a UC). I didn't realize the depth of this case until watching your presentation. So many aspects to this guy, but above all else, he was concretely mentally ill. 🤗🇲🇽

title: ENTER SANDMAN 🎸 What makes Virginia Tech's homefield advantage special | ACC Huddle
body: Met Lars at a restaurant in Truckee, Calif while at work.  I was leaving and glanced over at some people sitting at a booth.  As I walked outside, my mind kept trying figure out who that just was.  I hopped in my patrol car and called my brother, who's a drummer.  I said, "I think I just saw Lars Uhlrich."  I waited a few minutes and his group started coming outside.  I said, "hey Lars!"  He said, "hey man, whats going on?"  He then walked over to my car.  We chatted for a few minutes and I got an autograph.  It was a super cool experience... one Ill never forget!!  Same thing for David Coverdale.. met him in the same town at a chiropractors office.

title: Virginia Tech Victim Returns to Campus 12 Years After Being Shot | TODAY Originals
body: I was in fourth grade on this day in the community when that happened... Our school went on lock down, and we sat huddled in the corner, no talking, no lights. I was so young I didn't understand, but I remembered being scared because my mom worked near campus. Our community was eerily silent during this time, and for some time after. The community, love and support that has grown out of this horrible event, however, is such a beautiful and genuine feeling. If you ever visit, you will see it. I will never forget this day or those 32 that lost their lives. May their beautiful souls rest in peace. ❤️ We will prevail. We are Virginia Tech. 4/16/2007.

title: EVERYTHING You NEED to Know about VIRGINIA TECH
body:
Nice Vid man! I think I'm going to Virginia tech as a transfer student! VT is the only top School in VA (apart from W&M I believe) that has a Criminology/Criminal Justice degree and English literature which are two of the Majors I really want to study. I wanna be a Law Student and need the best most reputable school, with the best academic record to get into a really nice law School, I believe with the convenience of transfer, and the available Majors VT offers, It is perfect for me!

I live in NOVA, so the school is really far from home which is a bit scary ngl, but worth it. The school has a modern twist to Gothic architecture which I LOVE. Really excited to be going here! I am interested in STEM majors but for my career path I can't pursue it! I was thinking after Law School I could study some sort of stem major for my Doctoral Degree :)

title: What I wish I knew as a freshman at VIRGINIA TECH ✰ everything to know starting your 1st year at VT
body:
Loved watching this - excellent job! Definitely - BE excited. You'll remember these years as some of your best for the rest of your life. Work hard, play hard, but don't overdo either. I was a VT junior in 1976... Fun fact: In the 70's Tech was known as "VPI" - the name 'Virginia Tech' had not yet been officially adopted. Talking about my experience would have been like me hearing someone talk about their VT years from the 1920's - WOW! ANCIENT history.

For those who might be interested in Tech life 50 years ago... In 1976 Tech had a quarter system - fall, winter, spring, so you got a 'reset' three times a year, which I liked. No car Freshman year and you had to be on campus and eat dining hall food. No "plan" - you just ate there; HOWEVER, in the 7o's they built McDonald's and Burger King next to upper quad but I believe those have already been torn down. Our student IDs were more like a driver's license - no debit function (debit cards didn't exist yet) - and we really only used them to get in the dining halls. The food was okay but not great. Cigarettes were 43c a pack. Girls went to football games for the experience, guys went for the girls. It was 99% a social event. There was a ball game too. We chose classes for the next quarter by completing an "op scan" form - filling in little circles with a #2 pencil, presumably so a computer could read it. A few days after submission, we'd get snail mail letters with our class schedules. Since we never got ALL the classes we signed up for, we learned to sign up for more than we expected to take, then we could adjust later. There was no Internet, email, cell phones, or social media - all we did was face to face. The most high-tech things in the dorm room were the overhead light and a rotary phone. We loved having effectively no dress code for class, far more tolerant than it had been in high school. Home in Northern Virginia was 5 hours away and we'd go back for a weekend occasionally. People wanting to go home would sometimes post signs looking for rides or riders to help with gas costs. My roommate discovered that cars needing riders were always full so he tried contacting those looking for rides and always found availability - experience there teaches these "little tricks."

No doubt student life today would be very different and it would be fascinating to hear more experiences of today's students.
author: @HaveYouSeenThisOneVirginia

title: What I wish I knew as a freshman at VIRGINIA TECH ✰ everything to know starting your 1st year at VT
body: I went to Virginia Tech two decades ago.  Souvlaki is there on main street. I took karate at a dojo two buildings down and then went to have dinner at Souvlaki. They had lamb spinning inside the window. They would wrap wrap the sliced lamb , wrapped in pita bread with fresh vegetables and added homemade cucumber sauce. I always told them not to put in fresh onions as the onions overwhelm the taste of everything else.

title: WHY I TRANSFERRED COLLEGES | HIGH POINT UNIVERSITY to VIRGINIA TECH
body: My daughter is a senior in the interior design program at HPU, and the school and project have been nothing less than amazing! She received a internship in her field at the end of her sophomore year in her field! Also, had multiple firsthand experiences of designing things on campus! Everything she was "promised" on the tour has been true! I am thankful that she has had almost 4 years with no drama as we are seeing daily at other schools, been incredibly safe, and prepared for the "real world" after graduation! And full transparency we are a single parent family not at all a "country club" one. Not all schools are right for all students, so I think it is important to find the correct home for 4 years.
author: @adayinthelifeofkorri

title: WHY I TRANSFERRED COLLEGES | HIGH POINT UNIVERSITY to VIRGINIA TECH
body:
Anyone considering HPU please read:

I came across this video in my recommended, most likely because I’m going to be attending HPU in the fall. This was a great video but I did want to talk about some things that I feel like need to be said about hpu specifically and also just picking a school in general.

Firstly, like the video says, HPU is only 5k students which is considered a small school. And like you said, you end up seeing the same people all the time around campus. I can understand how that would be an issue but it all depends on the type of person you are. My friend is someone that just wants to be lost in a crowd of people at college and doesn’t want the whole school knowing who she is. Which is fine. But for me, I preferred the smaller school. I’m not the most outgoing person so for me to know that my name will be remembered by most people was a big part of my decision. It makes it easier to say hi to people and not worry that they won’t know who you are.

Second, the dorm situation. It is true that the nicer dorms cost more money but that doesn’t mean that if you don’t pay that, you’ll be in a bad dorm. On each of my tours, they showed us a couple different dorms, even the ones that were included in room and board and wouldn’t cost more money. On top of that, the extra cost to get the nice room shown in this video is not completely unreasonable. We’re not talking 10k extra.

Third, the food options, from what I’ve seen, are not limited. There are two Starbucks, the steakhouse, the Point grille, the cafe, great day bakery, the farmers market, the grille at the village, chick-fil-a, subway, jamba house, and the silver line diner. Not to mention the two mini supermarkets on campus. I can understand the desire to want to leave campus often for new food options but to say the choices there are limited, is a little unfair.

Fourth, there is no football team. That’s something that was told to us on our very first visit. But from what I’ve seen, they make up for it with their lacrosse and basketball teams. When I attended orientation a few months ago, we spent about an hour in the athletic center while the school put on a small show for their athletics, bringing out student athletes and the large pep band. I think what needs to be said here is that before deciding on a school, you really have to think about what you want. If you’re looking for thousands of people in stands with their faces painted the school’s colours, screaming and cheering on the team, then hpu might not be for you. But they definitely take pride in their athletics.

Fifth, there might not be a lot of name recognition to hpu now but it is an up-and-coming school. It sounds like you started freshman year there a couple of years ago and I’m sure not a lot of people knew where it was. While I was applying and thinking about it, there were a lot of people that knew of hpu. Some knew it from their lacrosse and baseball program. Others knew it on the corporate level from people applying for jobs. High point was only founded in 1924, making a relatively new college. After all, even Virginia Tech was once a new school that no one knew about.

Sixth, there are ways to cook in your dorm. A few different dorms do have kitchens in each suite and the newly constructed dorms have large, commercial sized kitchens at each end of the halls.

Seventh, there are more ways to get involved on campus other than the frats and sororities. I’ve spoken to many people that have been there for three years or longer that have said they didn’t join the Greek life and they had no trouble making friends and fitting in on campus.

Eighth, the sketchiness of the surrounding town isn’t the worst. Granted, it’s probably not as nice as VT’s town but it’s not like High Point is surrounded by a crime ridden area. Do you feel a little unsafe walking around town at night? Maybe. But everywhere has that. There are some really nice places in NYC but it is dangerous to walk around at night sometimes. But it is important to note that on campus, there is practically no danger. It is gated and there is security everywhere. There is also an app you can download that will alert you of any and all danger on and near campus.

I think it’s also important to note that I am from NY, my family is not rich by any means, and I could feel the school spirit as soon as I stepped onto campus. Like I said above, I’m going to be a freshman this fall so who knows. The things you said could be right. But it’s important not to forget that everyone is different. What some people find awful about the school could be someone’s favourite part about the school


Also, for the person that made this video: things could have changed since you attended, but these are more for the people that are considering hpu. I just figured it was only fair that people heard both sides.

title: WHY I TRANSFERRED COLLEGES | HIGH POINT UNIVERSITY to VIRGINIA TECH
body:
😂 omg, Kelsey I totally get ya and can relate. I also applied and got in to High Point, Catawba, Frostburg State, Eastern Carolina and Western Carolina, but chose West Virginia Wesleyan for it’s beautiful campus. I made some great friends and didn’t have problems with roommates, but ended up transferring after 2 years (91-93). Honestly it was like 13th and 14th grades in high school. There was a very high greek life and everyone was up in everyone’s business. It was hard to get private time with girlfriends or to study. My grades in high school were good (B+) but I didnt know what I wanted to do with my life. Being good in math I thought of economics or one of the sciences..but that all changed when I got a summer job working with a lawn care company. Turns out I’m not a 9-5 behind a desk guy. I loved being outside and seeing my work. So I applied to Tech and started fall 95.
When I transferred, Tech only took 9 of the 52 credits, but I figured it was worth it because I knew what I wanted. Now years I’m a self employed lawn/landscape business owner, making all those 8am Monday morning classes after a long weekend of partying worth it. 😂
School spirit was there at Wesleyan (both men, women’s soccer and baseball teams were good) but being in a stadium with 65,000 screaming fans while Enter Sandman gets blasted doesn’t compare to the maybe 500 fans we had at Wesleyan 😂
There’s also so much more to do and explore in Blacksburg. At Wesleyan, you’d either hang out in the Clarksburg Mall or at the bar. At Tech we had the Cascades, Duck pond, rafting on the New River and all sorts of fun activities on and off campus. Good times!
With winning teams, spirit goes up and I’m proud to say I was at the New Mexico State 🏀 game in Cassell in 1995 that sent us to the NIT championship at MSG vs Marquette, which we’d win to become the 65th best team in the country 😂
I also never missed a home football game as a student. I was also in New Orleans New Year’s Eve 1994 to celebrate a Sugar Bowl win vs University of Texas 28-10.. and even though we’d lose to FSU in the title game in 1999, being on campus during the Michael Vick years was electric! (Yeah I’m old, 😂)
Ps: sorry for the book 😂

title: WHY I TRANSFERRED COLLEGES | HIGH POINT UNIVERSITY to VIRGINIA TECH
body: Randomly stumbled across this video and I can’t believe our experiences were so similar despite being so many years apart. Except I loved the food options haha. I attended HPU from 2011-2012 and transferring after one year was the best decision I’ve EVER made. I felt incredibly out of place and like I didn’t fit in. It felt like everyone found their crew week #1 and if you didn’t you were stuck. I tried getting involved on campus but felt many of the students were entitled, arrogant etc. and they had no interest in studying. I ultimately became so depressed I stopped going to class and gained 40 pounds but fortunately still ended up with decent grades since the classes were easy. When I left I lost all the weight and started going to the gym and graduated with a 4.0 GPA. I think I needed to go to HPU for that year bcuz I bought into it and felt it was my “dream school” but it was not at all what I expected. I think I would’ve regretted it had I not gone. I also can’t imagine what it’s like if you aren’t conservative........

For awhile I made myself believe I had to stay despite all of the things done to me and that I experienced. But it’s been a year since I transferred and I’m so much happier and healthier. Also I am being challenged like never before I witnessed professors at HPU just passing students even when they were caught cheating. I worked for the school and the amount I found out behind the scenes and since I have left is so wild. title: WHY I TRANSFERRED COLLEGES | HIGH POINT UNIVERSITY to VIRGINIA TECH
body:
I randomly came across your post Kelsey and thought I should give y’all an overview of my time at my HPU because it mirrors the one you had. Before I begin let me preface that I graduated high school in 2009, and attended HPU for the 2009-10 year. Based on this video and from what I’ve heard from other people who have recently gone to HPU not much has changed there but some of what i’m saying could be a tad outdated.

HPU for sure has a small school feel. When I went there it had about 3,000 students. It was cliquey however sorority and fraternity rush was not until the spring back then. I struggled with severe homesickness my first semester and did not have the grades to rush a sorority second semester which was disappointing as all of the greek women I met in all houses were very welcoming and had so much pride for their house. However once all the girlfriends I made joined a sorority, I very rarely saw them, felt isolated and felt like I did not have a place at HPU because I was not greek. This is a school where you have to be greek or an athlete to fit in and anyone else gets lost in the social sauce pretty quickly. In the 9 years since I left there could be more clubs and social opportunities, but I’m certain greek life is still prominent.

I will say this; my second semester I was dealing with an unexpected medical situation and had to have surgery with a 3 week recovery. HPU was beyond kind and understanding. This further isolated me and was the icing on my cake to my choice to transfer (I wanted to transfer ever since first semester).

It’s so funny how everything happens for a reason (bad grades, health issues) because the school I ended up going to is beyond different than HPU and is not where I ever thought I would go to college. Just like Virginia Tech I went to a large state school with over 31,000 students near one of the safest towns in the USA and large school athletics.

I joined a sorority there (greek life is 2% of the population there), was part of community service clubs, but most of all made the most incredible life long friends. Once I got there the whole I love college thing clicked and the way I struggled at HPU never happened at my new school. Not one day passed where I thought wow I wish I had a steak house on campus or an arcade. As a woman in my late 20s, I truly believe I would not be where I am today if I had not transferred. It was the best choice I ever made.

Putting all the social/academic stuff aside, the biggest concern my parents and I had at HPU throughout my year there was the surrounding area. It is by no means safe and got communication from the school encouraging women to be as safe as possible when leaving campus. That’s all I am going to say in regards to that but if your someone who wants to be able to walk off campus and hit a local eatery or go for a run through your college town, HPU is not the place for that.

I was fortunate enough to have a financial situation that allowed me to be able to transfer and I get that’s not the case for everyone. However when making a choice about where to go to college, whether your a transfer or freshman, look past the surface level things and think about how you want your overall college experience to be.

title: The psychology of self-motivation | Scott Geller | TEDxVirginiaTech
body:
Quick summary:

3 Questions:
Do you believe you can do it? (have the time, knowledge and training)
Will it work? (takes education - why this might work)
Is it worth it? (motivation)

If yes to all three, you feel competent.
(training vs education: training involves feedback)

Choice - be mindful: be a success seeker rather than failure avoider, i.e. see things as opportunities rather than requirements

Community - support, have each other's back; cookie story

:)

title: The psychology of self-motivation | Scott Geller | TEDxVirginiaTech
body: I needed this video. I've been waking up every morning feeling terrified of the tasks that lay ahead of me. I'm 29 and in the second year of my masters degree. I woke up terrified because I felt I had little competence, little faith in positive consequences. I wasn't sure if what I was going to do was going to be worth it AND I felt that I was doing it all alone. It's no wonder I woke up scared! This talk taught me what I needed to feel self-motivated! If I don't have the competence I need to seem help and resources to help me learn. I can find faith in positive consequences AND rediscover my own reasons (why doing this is going to be WORTH it). And this morning, I already realized that I felt so much better when I acknowledged that it's not just me on my own doing this. I am surrounded by peers and teachers in every part of my life and I need to ask them to help me to achieve the outcomes by supporting and teaching me). This knowledge changes the way I experience my degree. I so desperately needed this. With this wisdom, I can wake up tomorrow not scared and anxious but confident and excited! Thank-you!

title: The psychology of self-motivation | Scott Geller | TEDxVirginiaTech
body:
I combine my current knowledge with the content from this speech to create a blueprint for intrinsic motivation. Please give it a read:
In order for one to be self-motivated, one needs to be equipped with a sense of:

1. Competent:
- What: Being empowered
- Why: You are motivated to do worthwhile work what you are competent of.
- How: Ask yourself 3 questions: > Can I do it? (If no, break it down step by step)
> Does it work/solve the problem? (If no, what changes/implementations/updates should/must be made)
> Is it worth it? (If no, reflect upon your choices and consequences of your choices in part 2 and 3)

2. Choice
- What: Being in autonomy
- Why: A sense of choice in your work in order to make it an intrinsic motivation.
- How: Make decision based on positive consequences and be mindful to consider your choices very thoroughly

3. Consequences:
- What: Looking forward to the outcome of the actions
- Why: Not feeling controlled by consequences in order to be in autonomy
- How: > Combine it with making choices, consider carefully if your choices are based on a positive or negative consequences.
> Being aware that autonomy comes from wanting positive outcomes, while control comes from avoiding negative outcomes.
> Make a choice to seek success (positive consequences) instead of to avoid failures/punishments (negative consequences).
> Sit back and reflect mindfully about the choices you have, being mindful about the consequence of your choices.

4. Community
- What: Seek interdepend and not independent nor codependent.
- How: > Understand a truthfully reflect on the value of oneself, while recognising your weaknesses
> Recognise what, when, and where you may need help by others.
> Learn to be patient and effective in communicating with others.
> Build a healthy self-esteem, being mindful about ego.
> Taking and accepting feedback mindfully, seeing feedback as a chance to improve competency (1) and not a negative criticism (3).

title: The psychology of self-motivation | Scott Geller | TEDxVirginiaTech

author: @marjoreal

title: The psychology of self-motivation | Scott Geller | TEDxVirginiaTech
body:
I've struggled with starting and keep doing something for a long time. But I found out that motivation is a myth that we keep telling ourselves we need in order to mask actual feelings and thoughts. You need:
1) A goal u want to achieve, anything can be a goal (ex: 2h of study per day), but the catch is that u must say if u can have exceptions to the rule or not. This will come in handy later, when u will have tricky thoughts like "I had a hard day and I deserve to finish early today because... (rationalization)".
2) A way to measure time because otherwise u will never have a start and an end. A schedule will do or my way is that I set a timer 25min before I do a task and then pomodoro 6/50 with 10 min breaks. Usually I take 25 min after 3 hours. Now u don't have to do this much and actually u shouldn't.
3) If u feel like a panic attack is coming when u think about having to sit down and start something it's most likely because the amygdala is making your body release too much cortisol in your body. I suggest u take some ashwagandha to lower it a bit.
4) There is a reason why mindfulness meditation works and it is because it teaches u to focus on your breath when thoughts surface out. I'm not sure if In my opinion it's really hard to make the difference between conscious thinking and pilot mode. Now the way u want to use this is by focusing on whatever else u can except your thoughts, especially when u have to go and do work. What I mean is focus on a sound or a picture, the wall, a chair, really anything but your thoughts. A lot of the pain comes from the dread that our mind tries to make us believe.
5) EXTRA: If u focus on your mind and try to see what the next thought that is going to come up is, u will see that none will surface.


This took me a long time to realize, and now I CAN do +6h/6day/week.

body: Personally $110 even for a small business is crazy, especially for such a simple design as well, if you have trouble getting clients then that is why, some people might like having a more personal nail artist but for most it doesn’t matter so they’d rather go to a salon and get the exact same quality and design for cheaper
author: @ivarozan2118

title: Day in the life of a Nail Tech WITH PRICES ✨🤭 #DITL #clermontnails #winternails #vbp #teamvbp
body: It`s always fascinating seeing American nail techs and their prices,because in Eastern Europe,I can comfortably get a set done(with intricate design etc that`ll last me a month) for 25 dollars at the MOST.
author: @AirAnimeAngel

title: Day in the life of a Nail Tech WITH PRICES ✨🤭 #DITL #clermontnails #winternails #vbp #teamvbp
body: $110? NOPE. I get my fill in and feet done for $60--85, depending on what I'm getting done every 2 weeks. I also order alot of my own stuff off of Amazon (stones, stickers, chrome powder, transfers, etc.). Its the EXACT SAME stuff that they order; it doesn't cost that much at all; and it keeps costs down at the salon bc they can't up-charge you for things that they didn't have to provide to you.
author: @jellybean3144

title: Day in the life of a Nail Tech WITH PRICES ✨🤭 #DITL #clermontnails #winternails #vbp #teamvbp
body: And that's why I do my own. Time consuming but worth it cause I'll be damned if I pay anyone over $60 for a set of nails.
author: @Titinefer1129

title: Day in the life of a Nail Tech WITH PRICES ✨🤭 #DITL #clermontnails #winternails #vbp #teamvbp
body: 2 CLIENTS at $110 and $130 for ONE DAY comes out to $240. If that was an 8 hour  shift it would be $30/hr. This person only had to do work twice in one day which was around 3-4 hours of work total, and had the remaining day off. Insane.
author: @KateLikesFries

title: Day in the life of a Nail Tech WITH PRICES ✨🤭 #DITL #clermontnails #winternails #vbp #teamvbp
body: I love how u 2 use the barter system to help each other. U do her nails and she does ur hair. Even exchange💱 GIRL POWER✊️💪💕
author: @TnCtv88

body: I mean you set the prices and if clients like the work you do and pay it then good for you 💕

title: Michael Vick: The Path to Greatness & the Long Road to Redemption | A Football Life
body:
39:56 how can you question his motives if he’s still doing the work even after he’s retired?

“Some people will never let you let go of your past”….I’m proud of Vick man…especially as a black man able to redeem himself
author: @CampionBondMUSIC

title: Michael Vick: The Path to Greatness & the Long Road to Redemption | A Football Life
body: I find it interesting that they involved PETA in this story when PETA itself has been found guilty of killing a multitude of animals over the years and continues to do so. Everyone deserves a second chance, everyone. Whether or not they do something positive with it is up to them, I think we can all see that Vick did that. I for one am glad to see that he was able to change his ways, humble himself and realize that it's not always about himself.
author: @DirtNasty1976

title: Michael Vick: The Path to Greatness & the Long Road to Redemption | A Football Life
body: The peta woman is weird, want do you want the man to do,  he served his time. He made the most out of his second chance.

title: Michael Vick: The Path to Greatness & the Long Road to Redemption | A Football Life
body: The most raw talented athlete that stepped onto the field im so happy I got to witness his athleticism 🙌🏿
author: @phillipthomas118

title: Michael Vick: The Path to Greatness & the Long Road to Redemption | A Football Life
body:
I don't think ppl give Mike enough credit for how mentally tough and morally straight he displayed once he got out of jail. All the headlines, hate and opposition is tough but he took full accountability and did all he could to make things right, opening a dog rehab center and getting back on the field.

Truly the definition of a comeback player. Cheers to the legend
author: @ryanm7087

title: Michael Vick: The Path to Greatness & the Long Road to Redemption | A Football Life
body: This dude was amazing. So talented. Vick was like the AI of the NFL.  I remember playing him on madden 2001 , it was an unfair advantage

body: "A Football Life" is my favorite sports documentary series to watch. The way that it's filmed really encapsulates the viewer into the shoes of what it's like to go from small town to superstar.

title: Michael Vick: The Path to Greatness & the Long Road to Redemption | A Football Life
body: I'm a Panthers fan, and was supposed to hate this dude for the way he abused our team as a division rival for years. Never could do it. Still can't. He did some dumb stuff, and he deserved to really pay. But I believe people deserve to redeem themselves, too. I'm happy to see Mike Vick has tried to do that for himself. I won't stop pulling for him. God bless this man.

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: Incredible to live in a time where you can get a direct glimpse into life at the White House from the President’s granddaughter on her YouTube vlog. Thank you, Kai.
author: @karlcarloshuderz

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: I respect how you make your own food, and do your own things without relying on others. Humble person, kind soul.
author: @numia4553

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: Kai trying to steam her clothing in the bathroom says all you need to know about how humble and unassuming this kid is. She's at the frickin WHITEHOUSE....I'm pretty sure they have laundry service and she could easily ask someone to get the wrinkles out of her clothes. I'm still amazed on how level headed she remains. Don't ever change Kai.
author: @kirby.hofman

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: Kai Trump is very respectful and clearly a good person.  And she interacts well with her grandfather in a quiet, respectful way too.
author: @herewegowithtim1984

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: It’s so cute watching her interact with Trump as her grandpa. You can tell he’s basically the same guy but softer and sweeter with her. He clearly loves his granddaughter so much❤
author: @Asdfghjkl-pb1ir

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: It’s incredible — never before has anyone shown what daily life inside the White House truly looks like. Your vlogs are making history, giving the world a rare glimpse into something we’ve only imagined. Truly remarkable initiative!
author: @TravelBro

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: I loved when the girls giggled and ran off after seeing their teacher in the restaurant! So “normal” and cute/innocent!
author: @beth4health2025

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: It’s such a privilege to be able to get glimpses of  DC and your time at the White House, as well as hearing the stories of  your past memories. Thank you Kai!
author: @elle366

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: She’s seems very down to earth and humble. Her voice is very laid back not high pitched and nasally. Also inviting her younger brother to be with her is so wholesome. It’s cute that she has him with her during her photoshoot. She doesn’t exclude him and makes sure he’s okay. She seems like a really good sister. It was sweet when he said he tried waiting for her when they ate dinner.
author: @Tttttrp-v1z

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: I love how we get to see not just ur grandpa's presidential voice but how he has such a soft grandpa voice, and then where the grandparents start pulling out the history books for everything like lights❤😂
author: @AlyssaAarmendariz

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: When I give my dogs medicine sometimes I try to have a treat or two to follow it up with to help with the bad after taste.  I really hope Bella is doing better.  🙏🏻❤️🙏🏻
author: @benicio1967

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: awww i think it's so sweet how your grandpa knocks. he treats you with so much love and respect :') your merch looks amazing too!
author: @ZZIKOish

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: Kai always thinks about her mom whenever she's shopping by getting her something sweet. This speaks volumes about who she is - respectful, kind, generous and grounded a reflection of the Trump family and its patriarch - President Trump - the greatest President of our time! Prayers for the entire Trump family 🙏🙏❤❤
author: @mark550-j3q

title: A day in my life at the White House
body: SPENCER!!!! SO great to see you here!! Thanks for letting Kai take some videos of you although they were like 2 seconds long.  We would love to see you more often!! Love all of you and your grandpa. :)
